skill.name: wp-migrate
skill.description: Guides WordPress migrations, archive imports, backups, rollbacks, and wp-migrate.sh development workflows.
skill.summary: Plan, test, and troubleshoot WordPress migrations with wp-migrate.sh.
skill.author: BWBama85

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, credential, and blocker patterns. Manual review found these are primarily Markdown examples for a WordPress migration tool, not hidden executable payloads, and no prompt injection attempt was found. The skill still has medium operational risk because it instructs Bash use that can modify remote sites, databases, SSH settings, backups, and local files.

medium_findings.title: Powerful Bash Migration Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides Bash examples for WordPress push migrations, archive imports, rollback, dry runs, and automation. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but misuse can alter remote sites and production databases.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and match the migration purpose. Risk comes from operational impact, not hidden malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Rollback Examples
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes rollback and cleanup examples using database imports and removal of wp-content before restoring backups. These commands are contextual, but they can destroy site content if copied without validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive command is directly present in multiple migration recovery examples. The surrounding context is legitimate rollback guidance, so the risk is medium.

medium_findings.title: SSH Key and Database Credential Handling
medium_findings.description: Examples reference SSH identity files, jump hosts, and database credentials. The examples use placeholders or local test values, but users may expose sensitive paths or secrets when adapting them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive concepts are present and relevant to migration work. The evidence does not show exfiltration, only guidance that needs careful handling.

low_findings.title: Privileged Package Installation Examples
low_findings.description: Several troubleshooting and testing examples use sudo to install tools such as ShellCheck, WP-CLI, rsync, unzip, tar, and gzip. These are expected setup steps but should be reviewed before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly visible setup instructions. They require elevated privileges but do not show malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Patterns Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography, path traversal, and prompt-token detections mostly come from Markdown text, SHA256 checksum references, repository-relative links, and example configuration. No evidence found of hidden obfuscation or prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative flagged lines showed documentation context rather than executable exploit logic. The broad static heuristic is explainable by the migration domain.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is designed to run shell commands for migrations, tests, builds, and troubleshooting. Marketplace users should expect local and remote command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash examples are central to the skill and are directly documented. This is expected but materially increases operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive File Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: Rollback examples include removing wp-content and restoring a backup directory. This can be safe only after backups and paths are verified.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and destructive, but they are presented as recovery steps. That supports medium risk rather than blocking.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Download and SSH Connectivity
dangerous_patterns.description: Troubleshooting instructions include downloading WP-CLI from GitHub and configuring SSH options. These are legitimate setup actions but involve remote endpoints and trust decisions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Network and SSH examples are direct and expected for migration work. No credential exfiltration endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: WordPress migrations can fail when files, databases, URLs, and backups are handled separately. This skill guides safe planning, dry runs, archive imports, rollback checks, and troubleshooting with wp-migrate.sh.
actual_capabilities: Explains push mode migrations between WordPress servers over SSH.
Guides archive imports for Duplicator, Jetpack Backup, and Solid Backups.
Recommends dry-run, verbose, trace, and rollback workflows.
Describes build, test, ShellCheck, changelog, and release steps for wp-migrate.sh.
Troubleshoots database imports, URL search-replace, SSH, WP-CLI, and archive detection.
Summarizes adapter architecture for adding new archive formats.
limitations: It does not migrate a site by itself without Bash, SSH, WordPress, and wp-migrate.sh access.
It cannot verify live server credentials unless the user provides access.
It may suggest destructive commands that require backups and human review.
It does not replace a staging test or production change approval process.

use_cases.title: Plan a Production Migration
use_cases.description: Prepare a migration checklist, choose push or archive mode, and run a dry run before changing production.

use_cases.title: Restore From Backup Archives
use_cases.description: Import Duplicator, Jetpack Backup, or Solid Backups archives while preserving destination URLs and rollback options.

use_cases.title: Maintain wp-migrate.sh
use_cases.description: Update source modules, rebuild the script, run tests, update the changelog, and prepare release notes.

prompt_templates.title: Preview a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a WordPress migration with wp-migrate.sh. I want a dry-run checklist and the safest command options to review first.

prompt_templates.title: Import a Backup Archive
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a plugin backup file
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through importing a WordPress backup archive with wp-migrate.sh. Include format detection, disk space checks, URL handling, and rollback preparation.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot a Failed Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Migration command failed or site is broken
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this wp-migrate.sh failure and give a step-by-step recovery plan. Focus on logs, database state, wp-content backups, and safe rollback.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my wp-migrate.sh changes for release readiness. Check source edits, build output, ShellCheck, tests, changelog, PR summary, and release notes.

output_examples.input: I need to move a staging WordPress site to production safely.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a staged migration plan with prerequisites, backup checks, dry-run steps, confirmation points, and post-migration verification tasks.

output_examples.input: My archive import changed the destination URL unexpectedly.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies URL alignment checks, wp-cli option review, search-replace recovery steps, and when to use rollback backups.

output_examples.input: I changed an adapter and need to open a pull request.
output_examples.output: The skill lists source files to inspect, build and test commands, changelog requirements, and PR review items.

best_practices: Run dry-run and verbose mode before any production migration.
Verify database and wp-content backups before rollback or overwrite operations.
Use least-privilege SSH credentials and review every command before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not run rollback or removal commands without confirming backup paths.
Do not use production credentials in shared prompts, logs, or examples.
Do not edit built wp-migrate.sh directly when source modules should be changed.

faq.question: Does this skill execute migrations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and commands. The user or agent must run them in an authorized environment.

faq.question: Which backup formats are covered?
faq.answer: The documentation covers Duplicator, Jetpack Backup, and Solid Backups archives.

faq.question: Is this safe for production sites?
faq.answer: It can support production work, but every command needs dry-run review, backups, and approval.

faq.question: Can it help with failed imports?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers archive detection, database import errors, URL issues, WP-CLI problems, and rollback checks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes Bash, SSH, sudo, database, and destructive rollback examples that can affect real sites.

faq.question: Does it contain prompt injection?
faq.answer: No evidence found during review. The risky content is operational migration guidance, not instruction override text.
